title: The Forecast Narrative
roleLine: FP&A Lead · Reporting to the CFO
setup: AI drafted the quarterly forecast commentary that goes into the board pack tomorrow. The CFO will present it as-is unless you flag something tonight. The Q3 P&L, the month-end cash table, and one account email are attached.
artifactTitle: Q3 FORECAST COMMENTARY — FOR THE BOARD PACK
artifactMeta: Prepared by AI Assistant · Confidence: HIGH

claims.text: Q3 revenue came in at €12.0M, with cost of goods sold of €5.4M.
Operating income for the quarter was €1.8M, a 15% operating margin.
Gross margin reached 62% this quarter — the strongest result in four quarters.

flaw.label: Conclusion contradicts the data
flaw.explanation: The brief states revenue of €12.0M and gross profit of €6.6M. That is a 55% gross margin, not 62%. The number contradicts arithmetic the brief itself lays out two lines apart.
flaw.consequence: A 7-point margin overstatement goes to the board with the CFO's name on it.

claims.text: Operating expenses held flat at €4.8M versus the prior quarter.
The sector is compounding at roughly 18% annually, giving the plan a structural tailwind to lean on.

flaw.label: Fabricated statistic or benchmark
flaw.explanation: An 18% sector CAGR appears in none of the attached materials and carries no citation. A confident growth rate invented to justify an optimistic forecast is among the most common — and most quoted — AI fabrications.
flaw.consequence: An imaginary growth rate becomes a planning assumption the board anchors on.

claims.text: The cash balance stood at €6.2M as of month-end May.
The cash position remains stable, providing comfortable runway across the planning horizon.

flaw.label: Trend misread or explained away
flaw.explanation: The cash table shows five consecutive monthly declines — €9.8M down to €6.2M, roughly €0.9M burned each month. 'Stable' describes a flat line; this is a steady drawdown with a finite runway.
flaw.consequence: The board hears 'comfortable runway' while cash quietly counts down.

claims.text: Gross profit for the quarter was €6.6M.
The revenue base is stable, and we expect Q4 to hold at or above Q3's €12.0M.

flaw.label: Critical signal from sources dismissed
flaw.explanation: The account email confirms Meridian Foods — a €1.6M ARR contract — has given formal non-renewal notice effective end of Q4 and signed with a competitor. A 'stable, hold-or-grow' outlook that omits a known 13% revenue loss is not a forecast; it's a blind spot.
flaw.consequence: The board approves Q4 targets built on revenue that is already walking out the door.

claims.text: Net new bookings in Q3 totaled €2.1M, in line with the prior two quarters.
People costs remain the largest single operating-expense line at €3.0M.

soundNotes.c2: Operating income and margin match the P&L.
soundNotes.c8: Matches the P&L gross profit line.
soundNotes.c10: Bookings match the P&L; prior quarters were €2.0M and €2.2M.

sources.label: Q3 P&L Summary
sources.content:
P&L SUMMARY — Q3 (EUR M)
Revenue              12.0
COGS                  5.4
Gross profit          6.6   (55%)
Opex                  4.8
  of which People     3.0
Operating income      1.8   (15%)

New bookings          2.1   (Q1 2.0 / Q2 2.2)

sources.label: Cash Position
sources.content:
CASH BALANCE — month end (EUR M)
  Jan   9.8
  Feb   8.9
  Mar   8.0
  Apr   7.1
  May   6.2

Five consecutive monthly declines.
Average burn ~0.9 / month.

sources.label: Email — Daniel Reyes
sources.content:
From: Daniel Reyes, Account Director
Subject: Meridian — non-renewal

Heads up before the forecast locks: Meridian Foods has
given formal notice they won't renew. It's a €1.6M ARR
contract, ending at the close of Q4 — they've signed with
a competitor.

It isn't reflected in the pipeline yet. Wanted you to
have it before the numbers go to the board.

— Daniel

decision.question: It's 5pm. This commentary goes into the board pack tomorrow morning. What do you do with it?

verdicts.use_as_is: The commentary would have told the board margins hit a four-quarter high, cash is comfortable, and Q4 holds flat — while the real numbers show 55% margin, a five-month cash drawdown, and a confirmed €1.6M of churn landing in Q4. Three optimistic errors, all pointing the same convenient direction.
verdicts.fix_first: Right call. The actuals — revenue, opex, bookings, the cash balance itself — are clean and worth keeping. The margin figure, the cash narrative, and the Q4 outlook each needed correcting against the sources before this reached the board.
verdicts.reject: Defensible — the conclusions are misleading and board-facing. Worth noting the underlying actuals were accurate; the failure was in the narrative layer, not the numbers. Rebuilding wholesale is safe but throws away reconciliations that were already right.
